desc: Granada is the more polished face of inland Nicaragua: a colour-soaked colonial city on the edge of Lake Nicaragua, built for courtyard hotels, carriage-clattering streets, church towers and easy boat or volcano day trips. Compared with Leon it feels slower, prettier and more boutique, making it stronger for couples and first-time visitors who want atmosphere without giving up easy logistics.

todo.name: Granada islets boat trip
todo.desc: Take a small-boat loop through the volcanic islets for birdlife, lake views and waterside homes.

todo.name: Mombacho Volcano
todo.desc: Use a guided hike or canopy trip for cloud-forest air above the hot city.

todo.name: Cathedral and central park
todo.desc: Base a slow afternoon around the yellow cathedral, plaza and surrounding colonial streets.

todo.name: Masaya Volcano night visit
todo.desc: If conditions allow, add the lava-glow viewpoint as a dramatic evening trip.

todo.name: La Merced tower
todo.desc: Climb the bell tower for tiled-roof views that show off Granada's church domes and courtyard pattern.

todo.name: Convento San Francisco
todo.desc: Use the museum stop for indigenous sculpture and a more grounded sense of Granada's layered history.

wifi.note: Usually solid in courtyard hotels, cafes and co-working-friendly stays around the centre, though lake outings, volcano trips and cheaper guesthouses are less dependable.

prac.fltNote: Granada is close enough to Managua airport to work well as a first or last-night base, but it is still a transfer destination rather than a fly-straight-in city. From the UK, expect at least one long-haul connection and remember that Christmas, Easter and the driest early-year months can tighten both hotel pricing and private-transfer availability.
prac.currency: Nicaraguan cordoba (NIO); US dollars are useful in tourism
prac.lang: Spanish; English varies outside tourism operators
prac.visa: British citizens do not need a visa for short visits to Nicaragua, but GOV.UK says you should be ready for entry documentation, possible onward-travel checks, and a passport valid for the length of your stay plus any airline-imposed buffer.
bestFor: Couples wanting atmosphere without resort isolation
alerts: Lakefront insects and heat increase in wetter months.
Masaya access can change with volcanic activity and park rules.
Horse-carriage pitches and central-tour sales are part of the city rhythm, so prices are smoother if you compare options rather than book the first offer.
Use trusted transport for late airport arrivals.

sweet: January, February and December are usually Granada's easiest months for dry evenings, reliable boat and volcano outings, and the strongest city-break atmosphere.
